tasks.title: Create src/services/agentSync.js
tasks.description: Implement the agentSync module with SHA-256 manifest-based safe sync. Exports syncAgents({ packageRoot, agentsDir, prismVersion, log }) → { synced, skipped, noChange, errors }. Implements the 3-case algorithm (Case 1: dest missing, Case 2: manifest entry present, Case 3: migration/no-baseline). Reads .prism-manifest.json at start of call; writes it atomically via .tmp→rename only if any entry changed. Tolerates: missing agents/ directory, malformed manifest JSON, per-file I/O errors (push to errors[], continue). Uses Node.js built-in crypto.createHash('sha256').
acceptance_criteria: syncAgents returns { synced, skipped, noChange, errors } with correct arrays for each scenario
Case 1: new file is copied to dest and manifest entry is written
Case 2a: dest matches manifest hash AND src equals dest → noChange, no disk write
Case 2b: dest matches manifest hash AND src differs → file updated, manifest entry updated
Case 2c: dest differs from manifest hash → file skipped (user-modified), no write
Case 3a: no manifest entry, src == dest → noChange, manifest entry written
Case 3b: no manifest entry, src != dest → file updated, manifest entry written, log 'first-sync updated'
Manifest written atomically via .tmp→rename
Missing agents/ directory → returns empty result without throwing
Malformed manifest JSON → treats as empty manifest, logs warning, does not throw
Per-file I/O error → pushed to errors[], remaining files processed normally
Module exports: { syncAgents, readManifest, writeManifest, computeHash } (helpers exported for testability)

tasks.title: Integrate syncAgents into server.js startServer()
tasks.description: Call syncAgents() inside startServer() after the PIPELINE_AGENTS_DIR resolution block. Add optional options.agentsDir field to startServer(options) for test injection. The agentsDir resolution order: options.agentsDir → process.env.PIPELINE_AGENTS_DIR → path.join(os.homedir(), '.claude', 'agents'). On silent mode, suppress sync logs. Log summary line '[agent-sync] synced N, skipped M' only when N>0 or M>0; log 'nothing to do (N agents up to date)' only at debug level (or suppress entirely to avoid startup noise). Errors are always logged regardless of silent mode.
acceptance_criteria: syncAgents() is called during startServer() before server.listen()
options.agentsDir is respected when passed to startServer()
PIPELINE_AGENTS_DIR env var is respected as fallback agentsDir
~/.claude/agents is used when neither option is set
Silent mode suppresses sync log lines
Errors from syncAgents are logged to console.error regardless of silent
Server startup proceeds normally even if syncAgents throws (try/catch around the call)

tasks.title: Update bin/init.js installAgents to write manifest
tasks.description: After the existing copy loop in installAgents(), write (or update) .prism-manifest.json in the destination directory. For installed files: compute srcHash after copy, write manifest entry with hash + prismVersion + syncedAt. For skipped files (already existed): compute srcHash from source and write manifest entry using srcHash — init asserts that the destination is Prism's baseline (either freshly installed or previously installed by init). This ensures the manifest exists from day 1 for fresh installs so auto-sync behaves correctly (Case 2 instead of Case 3) on the very first server start after init.
acceptance_criteria: .prism-manifest.json is created in agentsDir after prism init
Manifest has entries for all installed files with correct srcHash
Manifest has entries for skipped files using srcHash (not destHash)
installAgents returns { installed, skipped } with unchanged signature
Manifest write is atomic (.tmp→rename)
prism init is idempotent: running twice leaves manifest in consistent state
prism init --force still works correctly and updates manifest entries

tasks.title: Tests: tests/agent-sync.test.js
tasks.description: Comprehensive unit tests for src/services/agentSync.js. All tests use temp directories (mkdtempSync + cleanup in finally). Tests cover: the 3 sync cases, idempotency, manifest read/write, malformed manifest, missing agents/ dir, per-file error handling, and the installAgents manifest integration. Also add a test for the startServer() options.agentsDir injection path to verify the integration point without touching ~/.claude/agents.
acceptance_criteria: TC-001: empty agentsDir → all agents installed, manifest created with correct hashes
TC-002: second syncAgents call with unchanged source → all noChange, no disk writes
TC-003: source agent changed (simulate v2) → file updated, manifest hash updated
TC-004: user edits dest agent → skipped on next sync (manifest hash diverged)
TC-005: no manifest file exists, src == dest → noChange, manifest created
TC-006: no manifest file exists, src != dest → file updated (migration bias), logged
TC-007: missing agents/ source dir → returns { synced: [], skipped: [], noChange: [], errors: [] }
TC-008: malformed manifest JSON → treated as empty manifest, sync proceeds
TC-009: per-file read error → that file in errors[], other files processed normally
TC-010: prism init → .prism-manifest.json created with entries for all agents
TC-011: prism init idempotent → running twice does not corrupt manifest
TC-012: startServer options.agentsDir → sync targets that directory, not ~/.claude/agents
All tests pass: node --test tests/agent-sync.test.js

tasks.title: Update CHANGELOG.md
tasks.description: Add entry under ## [Unreleased] (or bump to 1.2.0) documenting the agent auto-sync feature: what changed (server startup now syncs agents automatically), the safe-sync guarantee (user customisations are preserved via SHA-256 manifest), the one-time migration note (first startup after upgrade updates existing agents without a manifest baseline), and the new log prefix [agent-sync] for observability.
acceptance_criteria: CHANGELOG.md has a new entry describing the auto-sync feature
Entry mentions the manifest file (.prism-manifest.json) and its location
Entry mentions the one-time migration behavior (first sync updates all agents)
Entry mentions that user-customised agents are protected on subsequent syncs
